name: PTC THERAPEUTICS, INC.
business: PTC Therapeutics is a global biopharmaceutical company dedicated to the discovery, development and commercialization of clinically differentiated medicines for children and adults living with rare disorders. Its commercial products include Sephience for PKU, Translarna and Emflaza for DMD, Upstaza/Kebilidi for AADC deficiency, Tegsedi and Waylivra for rare diseases, and Evrysdi for SMA.

exposures.sentence: We are a global biopharmaceutical company dedicated to the discovery, development and commercialization of clinically differentiated medicines for children and adults living with rare disorders.

catalysts.event: Sephience receives FDA approval for PKU
catalysts.sentence: In July 2025, Sephience was approved by the FDA for the treatment of pediatric and adult patients living with PKU in the United States age one month and above.

catalysts.event: Sephience receives Japanese approval for PKU
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, Sephience was approved by the Japanese Ministry of Health, Labor and Welfare, or MHLW, for the treatment of children and adults living with PKU in Japan.

catalysts.event: Sephience receives Brazilian approval for PKU
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, Sephience was approved by ANVISA, the Brazilian health regulatory authority, for the treatment of children and adults living with PKU in Brazil.

catalysts.event: Novartis expected to initiate global Phase 3 trial for votoplam
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, an End-of-Phase 2 meeting was held with FDA, and we reached alignment on design of a global Phase 3 clinical trial of votoplam, which we expect that Novartis will initiate in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: Results from Phase 2 PIVOT-HD extension study of votoplam expected
catalysts.sentence: We also expect to have results from the Phase 2 PIVOT-HD extension study of votoplam in the first half of 2026, once all participants cross the 24-month time point.

catalysts.event: FDA meeting to discuss vatiquinone study design
catalysts.sentence: We plan to meet with FDA in the second quarter of 2026 to discuss the design of this new study.

catalysts.event: Emflaza orphan drug exclusivity expires for ages 2 to less than 5
catalysts.sentence: Emflaza's orphan drug exclusivity related to the treatment of DMD in patients two years of age to less than five expires in June 2026.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 58,000 people living with PKU globally.
tamClaims.sentence: There are an estimated 58,000 people living with PKU globally.

tamClaims.claim: We estimate that there are between approximately 10,000 to 15,000 DMD patients in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that there are between approximately 10,000 to 15,000 DMD patients in the United States.

tamClaims.claim: The prevalence of AADC deficiency has been estimated to be approximately 5,000 patients worldwide, with a live-birth incidence of up to 1 in 40,000 worldwide.
tamClaims.sentence: The prevalence of AADC deficiency has been estimated to be approximately 5,000 patients worldwide, with a live-birth incidence of up to 1 in 40,000 worldwide.

tamClaims.claim: There are an estimated 50,000 patients with hATTR amyloidosis worldwide, including approximately 6,000 patients with polyneuropathic hATTR amyloidosis in Latin America.
tamClaims.sentence: There are an estimated 50,000 patients with hATTR amyloidosis worldwide, including approximately 6,000 patients with polyneuropathic hATTR amyloidosis in Latin America.

tamClaims.claim: We estimate that there are between 20,000 to 30,000 children and adults living with SMA in the United States, Europe and Japan.
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that there are between 20,000 to 30,000 children and adults living with SMA in the United States, Europe and Japan.

tamClaims.claim: We believe that there are approximately 135,000 HD patients globally.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that there are approximately 135,000 HD patients globally.

tamClaims.claim: We believe that there are approximately 25,000 FA patients globally, including approximately 6,000 in the United States.
tamClaims.sentence: We believe that there are approximately 25,000 FA patients globally, including approximately 6,000 in the United States.
